chapter_title.ar: باب مَنْ تُكْرَهُ الصَّلَاةُ خَلْفَهُ وَ الْعَبْدِ يَؤُمُّ الْقَوْمَ وَ مَنْ أَحَقُّ أَنْ يُؤَمَّ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 51 – The one behind whom the Salāt is disliked, and the slave leading the people, and the one who is rightful of leading (the Salāt)

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (5/258)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (5/258)
text: خَمْسَةٌ لَا يَؤُمُّونَ النَّاسَ عَلَى كُلِّ حَالٍ الْمَجْذُومُ وَ الْأَبْرَصُ وَ الْمَجْنُونُ وَ وَلَدُ الزِّنَا وَ الْأَعْرَابِيُّ .

en.hubeali: A group, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Al Husayn Bin Saeed, from Fazalat Bin Ayoub, from Al Husayn Bin Usman, from Ibn Muskan, from Abu Baseer,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullahasws having said: ‘Five cannot lead the people (in Salāt) upon every state – The leper, and the one with vitiligo, and the insane, and a son of adultery (bastard), and the Bedouin’.
en.sarwar: 1. A group has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from al-Husayn ibn Sa‘id from Fadalah ibn Ayyub from al-Husayn ibn ‘Uthman from ibn Muskan from abu Basir from who has said the following: “Abu ’Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ’Five kinds of people are not to lead other people in Salah (prayer) in all circumstances: People suffering from leprosy, insanity, one born out of wedlock and al-A‘ra’biy Arab of the desert.’”
